                 optional or not. The handling of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters can be overridden via
                   <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/>.
               </item>
             </list>
             All other parameter types are, by default, bound from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and are included in the generated JSON schema. This can be overridden by the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/> provided
             via the <paramref name="options"/> parameter; for every parameter, the delegate is enabled to choose if the parameter should be included in the
             generated schema and how its value should be bound, including handling of optionality (by default, required parameters that are not included in the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary will result in an exception being thrown). Loosely typed additional context information can be passed
             into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> via the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Context"/> dictionary; the default
             binding ignores this collection, but a custom binding supplied via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/> can choose to
             source arguments from this data.
             </para>
             <para>
             The default marshaling of parameters from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary permits values to be passed into the <paramref name="method"/>'s
             invocation directly if the object is already of a compatible type. Otherwise, if the argument is a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>, <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonDocument"/>,
             or <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode"/>, it is deserialized into the parameter type, utilizing <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> if provided,
             or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>. If the argument is anything else, it is round-tripped through JSON, serializing the object as JSON
             and then deserializing it to the expected type.
             </para>
             <para>
             In general, the data supplied via an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary is supplied from an AI service and should be considered
             unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of <paramref name="method"/>, consider having <paramref name="method"/>
             point to an instance method on an instance configured to hold the appropriate state. An <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameter can also be
             used to resolve services from a dependency injection container.
             </para>
             <para>
             By default, return values are serialized to <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> using <paramref name="options"/>'s
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> if provided, or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
             However, return values whose declared type is <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, a derived type of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, or
             any type assignable from <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> are not serialized and are instead returned directly.
             Handling of return values can be overridden via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/>.
             </para>
             <para>

             returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>. For methods returning
             <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>, no return schema is produced (the property is <see langword="null"/>).
             For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the schema is derived from the
             unwrapped result type. Return schema generation can be excluded via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
             and its generation is governed by <paramref name="options"/>'s <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> represents an instance method but <paramref name="target"/> is null.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="method"/> represents an open generic method.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="method"/> contains a parameter without a parameter name.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonException">A parameter to <paramref name="method"/> or its return type is not serializable.</exception>

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Reflection.MethodInfo,System.Object,System.String,System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
             <summary>
             Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via an <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> instance
             and an optional target object if the method is an instance method.
             </summary>
             <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
             <param name="target">

             any <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DisplayNameAttribute"/> on <paramref name="method"/>, if available, or else from the name of <paramref name="method"/>.

                 the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is
                 not included in the generated JSON schema.
               </item>
               <item>
                 By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
                 and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
                 must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.
               </item>
               <item>

                 passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and are not included in the JSON schema. If the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>
                 instance passed to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is <see langword="null"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> implementation
                 manufactures an empty instance, such that parameters of type <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> can always be satisfied, whether
                 optional or not.
               </item>
             </list>
             All other parameter types are bound from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and are included in the generated JSON schema.
             </para>
             <para>
             The marshaling of parameters from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary permits values to be passed into the <paramref name="method"/>'s
             invocation directly if the object is already of a compatible type. Otherwise, if the argument is a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>, <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonDocument"/>,
             or <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonNode"/>, it is deserialized into the parameter type, utilizing <paramref name="serializerOptions"/> if provided, or else
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>. If the argument is anything else, it is round-tripped through JSON, serializing the object as JSON
             and then deserializing it to the expected type.
             </para>
             <para>
             In general, the data supplied via an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary is supplied from an AI service and should be considered
             unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of <paramref name="method"/>, consider having <paramref name="method"/>
             point to an instance method on an instance configured to hold the appropriate state. An <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameter can also be
             used to resolve services from a dependency injection container.
             </para>
             <para>
             Return values are serialized to <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> using <paramref name="serializerOptions"/> if provided,
             or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>. However, return values whose declared type is <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, a
             derived type of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, or any type assignable from <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> are returned
             without serialization to enable specialized handling.
             </para>
             <para>
             A JSON schema is also derived from the method's return type and exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>.
             For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>, no return schema is produced.
             For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the schema is derived from the unwrapped result type.

4769.             <summary>
4770.             Represents options that can be provided when creating an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> from a method.
4771.             </summary>
4772.         </member>
4773.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.#ctor">
4774.             <summary>

4776.             </summary>
4777.         </member>
4778.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions">
4779.             <summary>Gets or sets the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> used to marshal .NET values being passed to the underlying delegate.</summary>
4780.             <remarks>
4781.             If no value has been specified, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/> instance will be used.
4782.             </remarks>
4783.         </member>
4784.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions">
4785.             <summary>
4786.             Gets or sets the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions"/> governing the generation of JSON schemas for
4787.             the function's input parameters and return type.
4788.             </summary>
4789.             <remarks>
4790.             If no value has been specified, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions.Default"/> instance will be used.
4791.             This setting affects both the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/> (input parameters) and
4792.             the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/> (return type).
4793.             </remarks>
4794.         </member>

4796.             <summary>Gets or sets the name to use for the function.</summary>
4797.             <value>
4798.             The name to use for the function. The default value is a name derived from the passed <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> (for example, via a <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DisplayNameAttribute"/> on the method).
4799.             </value>
4800.         </member>
4801.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.Description">
4802.             <summary>Gets or sets the description to use for the function.</summary>
4803.             <value>
4804.             The description for the function. The default value is a description derived from the passed <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>, if possible
4805.             (for example, via a <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/> on the method).
4806.             </value>
4807.         </member>

4811.             </summary>
4812.             <remarks>

4816.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding">
4817.             <summary>Gets or sets a delegate used to determine how a particular parameter to the function should be bound.</summary>
4818.             <remarks>
4819.             <para>
4820.             If <see langword="null"/>, the default parameter binding logic will be used. If non-<see langword="null"/> value,
4821.             this delegate will be invoked once for each parameter in the function as part of creating the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance.
4822.             It is not invoked for parameters of type <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/>, which are invariably bound to the token
4823.             provided to the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> invocation.
4824.             </para>
4825.             <para>

4831.         <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult">
4832.             <summary>Gets or sets a delegate used to determine the <see cref="T:System.Object"/> returned by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.</summary>
4833.             <remarks>
4834.             <para>
4835.             By default, the return value of invoking the method wrapped into an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> by <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/>
4836.             is then JSON serialized, with the resulting <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method.
4837.             This default behavior is ideal for the common case where the result will be passed back to an AI service. However, if the caller
4838.             requires more control over the result's marshaling, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> property may be set to a delegate that is
4839.             then provided with complete control over the result's marshaling. The delegate is invoked with the value returned by the method,
4840.             and its return value is then returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method.
4841.             </para>
4842.             <para>
4843.             When set, the delegate is invoked even for <see langword="void"/>-returning methods, in which case it is invoked with
4844.             a <see langword="null"/> argument. By default, <see langword="null"/> is returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
4845.             method for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances produced by <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> to wrap
4846.             <see langword="void"/>-returning methods).
4847.             </para>
4848.             <para>
4849.             Methods strongly typed to return types of <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
4850.             and <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/> are special-cased. For methods typed to return <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
4851.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> will be invoked with the <see langword="null"/> value after the returned task has successfully completed.
4852.             For methods typed to return <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the delegate will be invoked with the
4853.             task's result value after the task has successfully completed.These behaviors keep synchronous and asynchronous methods consistent.
4854.             </para>
4855.             <para>
4856.             In addition to the returned value, which is provided to the delegate as the first argument, the delegate is also provided with
4857.             a <see cref="T:System.Type"/> represented the declared return type of the method. This can be used to determine how to marshal the result.
4858.             This may be different than the actual type of the object (<see cref="M:System.Object.GetType"/>) if the method returns a derived type
4859.             or <see langword="null"/>. If the method is typed to return <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>, or <see langword="void"/>,
4860.             the <see cref="T:System.Type"/> argument will be <see langword="null"/>.
4861.             </para>
4862.             </remarks>
4863.         </member>

4872.             <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>.
4873.             </para>

4884.             <remarks>

             <summary>Gets or sets a delegate used to determine how a particular parameter to the function should be bound.</summary>
             <remarks>

             this delegate will be invoked once for each parameter in the function as part of creating the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance.
             It is not invoked for parameters of type <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/>, which are invariably bound to the token
             provided to the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> invocation.
             </para>
             <para>

             </para>
             </remarks>

             <summary>Gets or sets a delegate used to determine the <see cref="T:System.Object"/> returned by <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.</summary>

             is then JSON serialized, with the resulting <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method.
             This default behavior is ideal for the common case where the result will be passed back to an AI service. However, if the caller
             requires more control over the result's marshaling, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> property may be set to a delegate that is
             then provided with complete control over the result's marshaling. The delegate is invoked with the value returned by the method,
             and its return value is then returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method.
             </para>
             <para>
             When set, the delegate is invoked even for <see langword="void"/>-returning methods, in which case it is invoked with
             a <see langword="null"/> argument. By default, <see langword="null"/> is returned from the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             method for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances produced by <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> to wrap
             <see langword="void"/>-returning methods).
             </para>
             <para>
             Methods strongly typed to return types of <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
             and <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/> are special-cased. For methods typed to return <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> will be invoked with the <see langword="null"/> value after the returned task has successfully completed.
             For methods typed to return <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the delegate will be invoked with the
             task's result value after the task has successfully completed.These behaviors keep synchronous and asynchronous methods consistent.
             </para>
             <para>
             In addition to the returned value, which is provided to the delegate as the first argument, the delegate is also provided with
             a <see cref="T:System.Type"/> represented the declared return type of the method. This can be used to determine how to marshal the result.
             This may be different than the actual type of the object (<see cref="M:System.Object.GetType"/>) if the method returns a derived type

             </para>

            <summary>Represents a tool that can be specified to an AI service.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.#ctor">
            <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/> class.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.Name">
            <summary>Gets the name of the tool.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.Description">
            <summary>Gets a description of the tool, suitable for use in describing the purpose to a model.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.AdditionalProperties">
            <summary>Gets any additional properties associated with the tool.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.ToString">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.GetService(System.Type,System.Object)">
            <summary>Asks the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/> for an object of the specified type <paramref name="serviceType"/>.</summary>
            <param name="serviceType">The type of object being requested.</param>
            <param name="serviceKey">An optional key that can be used to help identify the target service.</param>
            <returns>The found object, otherwise <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="serviceType"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
            <remarks>
            The purpose of this method is to allow for the retrieval of strongly typed services that might be provided by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/>,
            including itself or any services it might be wrapping.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.GetService``1(System.Object)">
            <summary>Asks the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/> for an object of type <typeparamref name="TService"/>.</summary>

            <returns>The found object, otherwise <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
            <remarks>
            The purpose of this method is to allow for the retrieval of strongly typed services that may be provided by the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool"/>,
            including itself or any services it might be wrapping.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.DebuggerDisplay">
            <summary>Gets the string to display in the debugger for this instance.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.HostedCodeInterpreterTool">
            <summary>Represents a hosted tool that can be specified to an AI service to enable it to execute code it generates.</summary>
            <remarks>
            This tool does not itself implement code interpretation. It is a marker that can be used to inform a service
            that the service is allowed to execute its generated code if the service is capable of doing so.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.HostedCodeInterpreterTool._additionalProperties">
            <summary>Any additional properties associated with the tool.</summary>
<exited with exit code 0>

            <summary>
            Gets the underlying <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> that this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> might be wrapping.
            </summary>
            <remarks>

            </remarks>

        </member>

            <summary>Invokes the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> and returns its result.</summary>
            <param name="arguments">The arguments to pass to the function's invocation.</param>

        </member>

            <returns>The result of the function's execution.</returns>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.AsDeclarationOnly">
            <summary>Creates a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> representation of this <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> that can't be invoked.</summary>
            <returns>The created instance.</returns>
            <remarks>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> derives from <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/>, layering on the ability to invoke the function in addition
            to describing it. <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.AsDeclarationOnly"/> creates a new object that describes the function but that can't be invoked.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments">
            <summary>Represents arguments to be used with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> is a dictionary of name/value pairs that are used
            as inputs to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>. However, an instance carries additional non-nominal
            information, such as an optional <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> that can be used by
            an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> if it needs to resolve any services from a dependency injection
            container.
            </remarks>
        </member>

        </member>

1203.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
1204.             <summary>
1205.             A delegating chat client that invokes functions defined on <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/>.
1206.             Include this in a chat pipeline to resolve function calls automatically.
1207.             </summary>
1208.             <remarks>
1209.             <para>
1210.             When this client receives a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in a chat response from its inner
1211.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, it responds by invoking the corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> defined
1212.             in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> (or in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>), producing a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
1213.             that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until
1214.             another stop condition is met, such as hitting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest"/>.
1215.             </para>
1216.             <para>
1217.             If a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> but not an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>, the
1218.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
1219.             to pass back out to the caller. It is then that caller's responsibility to create the appropriate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
1220.             for that call and send it back as part of a subsequent request.
1221.             </para>
1222.             <para>
1223.             Further, if a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not
1224.             attempt to invoke it directly. Instead, it will replace that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalRequestContent"/>
1225.             that wraps the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> and indicates that the function requires approval before it can be invoked. The caller is then
1226.             responsible for responding to that approval request by sending a corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalResponseContent"/> in a subsequent
1227.             request. The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will then process that approval response and invoke the function as appropriate.
1228.             </para>
1229.             <para>
1230.             Due to the nature of interactions with an underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, if any <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> is received
1231.             for a function that requires approval, all received <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in that same response will also require approval,
1232.             even if they were not <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/> instances. If this is a concern, consider requesting that multiple tool call
1233.             requests not be made in a single response, by setting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowMultipleToolCalls"/> to <see langword="false"/>.
1234.             </para>
1235.             <para>
1236.             A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> instance is thread-safe for concurrent use so long as the
1237.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances employed as part of the supplied <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> are also safe.
1238.             The <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> property can be used to control whether multiple function invocation
1239.             requests as part of the same request are invocable concurrently, but even with that set to <see langword="false"/>
1240.             (the default), multiple concurrent requests to this same instance and using the same tools could result in those
1241.             tools being used concurrently (one per request). For example, a function that accesses the HttpContext of a specific
1242.             ASP.NET web request should only be used as part of a single <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> at a time, and only with
1243.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> set to <see langword="false"/>, in case the inner client decided to issue multiple
1244.             invocation requests to that same function.
1245.             </para>
1246.             </remarks>
1247.         </member>

         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient">
             <summary>
             A delegating chat client that invokes functions defined on <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/>.
             Include this in a chat pipeline to resolve function calls automatically.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When this client receives a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in a chat response from its inner
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, it responds by invoking the corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> defined
             in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools"/> (or in <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools"/>), producing a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
             that it sends back to the inner client. This loop is repeated until there are no more function calls to make, or until
             another stop condition is met, such as hitting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.MaximumIterationsPerRequest"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             If a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration"/> but not an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>, the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not attempt to invoke it, and instead allow that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/>
             to pass back out to the caller. It is then that caller's responsibility to create the appropriate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionResultContent"/>
             for that call and send it back as part of a subsequent request.
             </para>
             <para>
             Further, if a requested function is an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will not
             attempt to invoke it directly. Instead, it will replace that <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalRequestContent"/>
             that wraps the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> and indicates that the function requires approval before it can be invoked. The caller is then
             responsible for responding to that approval request by sending a corresponding <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ToolApprovalResponseContent"/> in a subsequent
             request. The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> will then process that approval response and invoke the function as appropriate.
             </para>
             <para>
             Due to the nature of interactions with an underlying <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>, if any <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> is received
             for a function that requires approval, all received <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent"/> in that same response will also require approval,
             even if they were not <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ApprovalRequiredAIFunction"/> instances. If this is a concern, consider requesting that multiple tool call
             requests not be made in a single response, by setting <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowMultipleToolCalls"/> to <see langword="false"/>.
             </para>
             <para>
             A <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient"/> instance is thread-safe for concurrent use so long as the
             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances employed as part of the supplied <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> are also safe.
             The <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> property can be used to control whether multiple function invocation
             requests as part of the same request are invocable concurrently, but even with that set to <see langword="false"/>
             (the default), multiple concurrent requests to this same instance and using the same tools could result in those
             tools being used concurrently (one per request). For example, a function that accesses the HttpContext of a specific
             ASP.NET web request should only be used as part of a single <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions"/> at a time, and only with
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient.AllowConcurrentInvocation"/> set to <see langword="false"/>, in case the inner client decided to issue multiple
             invocation requests to that same function.
             </para>
             </remarks>

<member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools">
            <summary>Gets or sets the list of tools to include with a chat request.</summary>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#tool-calling">Tool calling.</related>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.AllowBackgroundResponses">
            <summary>Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background responses are allowed.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            Background responses allow running long-running operations or tasks asynchronously in the background that can be resumed by streaming APIs
            and polled for completion by non-streaming APIs.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to <see langword="true" />, non-streaming APIs have permission to start a background operation and return an initial
            response with a continuation token. Subsequent calls to the same API should be made in a polling manner with
            the continuation token to get the final result of the operation.
            </para>
            <para>
            When this property is set to <see langword="true" />, streaming APIs are also permitted to start a background operation and begin streaming
            response updates until the operation is completed. If the streaming connection is interrupted, the
            continuation token obtained from the last update that has one should be supplied to a subsequent call to the same streaming API
            to resume the stream from the point of interruption and continue receiving updates until the operation is completed.
            </para>
            <para>
            This property only takes effect if the implementation it's used with supports background responses.
            If the implementation does not support background responses, this property will be ignored.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.ContinuationToken">
            <summary>Gets or sets the continuation token for resuming and getting the result of the chat response identified by this token.</summary>
            <remarks>

         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ImageGeneratingChatClient">
             <summary>A delegating chat client that enables image generation capabilities by converting <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.HostedImageGenerationTool"/> instances to function tools.</summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The provided implementation of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> is thread-safe for concurrent use so long as the

             <para>

             and replaces them with equivalent function tools that the chat client can invoke to perform image generation and editing operations.

             <summary>

             </summary>

             Use this enumeration to control whether images in the data content are passed as-is, replaced
             with unique identifiers, or only generated images are replaced. This setting affects how downstream clients
             receive and process image data.
             Reducing what's passed downstream can help manage the context window.

             is supplied without <paramref name="getStreamingResponseFunc"/>, the implementation of <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             will employ limited streaming, as it will be operating on the batch output produced by <paramref name="getResponseFunc"/>. And if
             <paramref name="getStreamingResponseFunc"/> is supplied without <paramref name="getResponseFunc"/>, the implementation of
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will be implemented by combining the updates from <paramref name="getStreamingResponseFunc"/>.
             </remarks>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException">Both <paramref name="getResponseFunc"/> and <paramref name="getStreamingResponseFunc"/> are <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/microsoft-extensions-ai#functionality-pipelines">Pipelines of functionality.</related>

            <summary>
            Represents the response to an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> run request, containing messages and metadata about the interaction.

<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession">
            <summary>
            Base abstraction for all agent threads.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> contains the state of a specific conversation with an agent which may include:
            <list type="bullet">
            <item><description>Conversation history or a reference to externally stored conversation history.</description></item>
            <item><description>Memories or a reference to externally stored memories.</description></item>
            <item><description>Any other state that the agent needs to persist across runs for a conversation.</description></item>
            </list>
            </para>
            <para>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may also have behaviors attached to it that may include:
            <list type="bullet">
            <item><description>Customized storage of state.</description></item>
            <item><description>Data extraction from and injection into a conversation.</description></item>
            <item><description>Chat history reduction, e.g. where messages needs to be summarized or truncated to reduce the size.</description></item>
            </list>
            An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> is always constructed by an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> so that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>
            can attach any necessary behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/>. See the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.CreateSessionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
            and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.DeserializeSessionAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> methods for more information.
            </para>
            <para>
            Because of these behaviors, an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may not be reusable across different agents, since each agent
            may add different behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> it creates.
            </para>
            <para>
            To support conversations that may need to survive application restarts or separate service requests, an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> can be serialized
            and deserialized, so that it can be saved in a persistent store.

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a complete summary document:

## Microsoft.Agents.AI NuGet Package (v1.3.0) - Agentic Workflow API Reference

### **Core Architecture**

The framework provides a **tool-calling agent loop pattern** where agents orchestrate conversations with LLMs and automatically dispatch tool calls. The architecture follows this flow:

```
Messages → Agent.Run() → LLM Chat Call → Tool Dispatch (if needed) → Loop → Response
```

---

### **1. Main Agent Classes**

#### **AIAgent** (Base Class)
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent`  
**File:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.3.0/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.xml`

**Key Properties:**
- `Id: string` - Unique agent identifier
- `IdCore: string?` - Custom identifier (can be overridden by derived classes)

**Core Methods:**
```csharp
// Run the agent with various input types
RunAsync(AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): Task<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(string message, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): Task<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(ChatMessage, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): Task<AgentResponse>
RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): Task<AgentResponse>

// Streaming version
RunStreamingAsync(...): IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>

// Core implementation (must override in derived classes)
RunCoreAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken): Task<AgentResponse>

// Session management
CreateSessionAsync(CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentSession>
SerializeSessionAsync(AgentSession, JsonSerializerOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<string>
DeserializeSessionAsync(JsonElement, JsonSerializerOptions, CancellationToken): ValueTask<AgentSession>
```

---

#### **ChatClientAgent**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent`  
**File:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.xml`

**Purpose:** Delegates to an `IChatClient` implementation; orchestrates the agent loop with automatic tool invocation.

**Constructors:**

```csharp
// Constructor 1: Basic initialization
ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    string? instructions = null,
    string? name = null,
    string? description = null,
    IList<AITool>? tools = null,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null
)

// Constructor 2: Options-based initialization
ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    ChatClientAgentOptions options,
    ILoggerFactory? loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider? services = null
)
```

**Key Properties:**
- `ChatClient: IChatClient` - The underlying LLM chat client
- `ChatHistoryProvider: ChatHistoryProvider?` - External history storage provider
- `AIContextProviders: IReadOnlyList<AIContextProvider>?` - Context enrichment providers
- `Instructions: string?` - System instructions for the agent
- `ChatOptions: ChatOptions` - Default chat options
- `Name: string?` - Agent name
- `Description: string?` - Agent description

**Key Methods:**
```csharp
// Override the core agent loop
RunCoreAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)
RunCoreStreamingAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)

// Create and manage sessions
CreateSessionAsync(string conversationId, CancellationToken)
CreateSessionCoreAsync(CancellationToken)

// Advanced configuration
ApplyRunOptionsTransformations(AgentRunOptions, IChatClient): IChatClient

// Internal methods for hooks
NotifyProvidersOfNewMessagesAsync(...)
NotifyProvidersOfFailureAsync(...)
```

---

### **2. Configuration Classes**

#### **ChatClientAgentOptions**
**Properties:**
```csharp
Id: string?                           // Agent ID
Name: string?                         // Agent name
Description: string?                 // Agent description
ChatOptions: ChatOptions?             // Default chat options (temperature, max_tokens, etc.)
ChatHistoryProvider: ChatHistoryProvider?  // External history storage
AIContextProviders: IList<AIContextProvider>?  // Context providers for enrichment

// Advanced
UseProvidedChatClientAsIs: bool       // Skip default decorators if true
ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict: bool // Clear provider if service manages history
```

#### **ChatClientAgentRunOptions**
**Inherits from:** `AgentRunOptions`  
**Properties:**
```csharp
ChatOptions: ChatOptions?             // Per-run chat options (override defaults)
ChatClientFactory: Func<IChatClient, IChatClient>?  // Custom chat client decorator/wrapper
```

#### **AgentRunOptions**
**Properties:**
```csharp
ContinuationToken: string?            // For resuming background operations
AllowBackgroundResponses: bool        // Enable async/streaming responses
```

---

### **3. Session Management**

#### **AgentSession**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession`

**Purpose:** Encapsulates conversation state (history, memories, custom state).

**Key Features:**
- Holds chat history or reference to external history storage
- Maintains memories and conversation state
- Can be serialized/deserialized for persistence
- May have behaviors attached (history reduction, data extraction)

#### **ChatClientAgentSession**
**Derived from:** `AgentSession`

**Key Properties:**
```csharp
ConversationId: string?  // For service-side managed chat history
```

---

### **4. Tool/Function System**

#### **AITool**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool`  
**File:** `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/10.5.0/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions.xml`

**Properties:**
```csharp
Name: string              // Tool name
Description: string       // Tool description (for model)
AdditionalProperties: IDictionary<string, object>?  // Extra metadata
```

**Methods:**
```csharp
GetService(Type serviceType, object? serviceKey): object?
GetService<TService>(object? serviceKey): TService?
```

---

#### **AIFunction**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction`  
**Inherits from:** `AIFunctionDeclaration`

**Purpose:** Represents an invokable function that LLMs can call.

**Key Properties:**
```csharp
UnderlyingMethod: MethodInfo?        // The wrapped .NET method (if any)
JsonSerializerOptions: JsonSerializerOptions  // For marshaling parameters
```

**Key Methods:**
```csharp
InvokeAsync(AIFunctionArguments, CancellationToken): Task<object?>
InvokeCoreAsync(AIFunctionArguments, CancellationToken): Task<object?>  // Override point
AsDeclarationOnly(): AIFunctionDeclaration  // Get declaration-only copy
```

---

#### **AIFunctionArguments**
**Purpose:** Dictionary-like container for function call parameters.

**Constructors:**
```csharp
AIFunctionArguments()  // Empty
AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object> arguments)  // With args
AIFunctionArguments(IEqualityComparer<string> comparer)  // Custom comparer
AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object>, IEqualityComparer<string>)
```

**Key Properties:**
```csharp
Services: IServiceProvider?           // DI container for function use
Context: IDictionary<string, object>? // Arbitrary context data
Item[string key]: object?             // Dictionary-like access
```

---

#### **ChatOptions.Tools**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions`  
**Type:** `IList<AITool>`

**Purpose:** Attach tools to a specific chat invocation.

```csharp
var options = new ChatOptions
{
    Tools = new List<AITool>
    {
        myFunction1,
        myFunction2,
        // ...
    }
};
```

---

#### **FunctionInvokingChatClient**
**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient` (implied from decorators)

**Role in Framework:**
- Automatically invokes functions when LLM requests them
- Loops until LLM stops requesting tool calls
- Returns final assistant response or intermediate tool results
- Typically inserted by `ChatClientAgent` if not already present

---

### **5. Response Types**

#### **AgentResponse**
**Constructors:**
```csharp
AgentResponse()
AgentResponse(ChatMessage message)
AgentResponse(ChatResponse response)  // Wraps ChatResponse
AgentResponse(AgentResponse response)  // Copy constructor
```

**Key Properties:**
```csharp
Messages: IReadOnlyList<ChatMessage>  // All response messages
Text: string?                         // First message text (convenience)
RawRepresentation: object?            // Original response from service
```

---

#### **AgentResponseUpdate**
**Purpose:** For streaming responses - each update from the agent stream.

---

### **6. Agent Builder Pattern**

#### **AIAgentBuilder**
**Purpose:** Build a pipeline of agent decorators.

**Constructors:**
```csharp
AIAgentBuilder(AIAgent innerAgent)
AIAgentBuilder(Func<IServiceProvider, AIAgent> innerAgentFactory)
```

**Key Methods:**
```csharp
// Add middleware/decorator layers
Use(Func<AIAgent, AIAgent> agentFactory): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<AIAgent, IServiceProvider, AIAgent> agentFactory): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, AgentRunOptions, 
        Func<...>, CancellationToken, Task> sharedFunc): AIAgentBuilder
Use(Func<..., Task<AgentResponse>>, Func<..., IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>>): AIAgentBuilder

// Finalize and return
Build(IServiceProvider? services): AIAgent
```

---

### **7. Context & Provider System**

#### **AIContextProvider**
**Purpose:** Enriches agent invocations with additional context, messages, tools, or instructions.

**Workflow:**
- Called before each chat completion
- Can inject additional context (messages, tools, system instructions)
- Called again after completion for cleanup/notifications

**Nested Types:**
```csharp
InvokingContext  // Pre-call context
InvokedContext   // Post-call context
```

#### **ChatHistoryProvider**
**Purpose:** Manages external chat history storage (instead of in-memory).

---

### **8. The Agent Loop Pattern**

**Simplified Pseudocode:**
```
Session = CreateSession()

repeat:
    Messages = GetInputMessages()
    
    // Load history
    FullMessages = LoadChatHistory(Session) + Messages
    
    // Invoke LLM
    Response = CallLLM(FullMessages, Tools=AllRegisteredTools)
    
    // Dispatch loop
    while Response.Message.Type == "ToolCall":
        foreach ToolCall in Response.Message.ToolCalls:
            ToolResult = InvokeTool(ToolCall)
            FullMessages.Add(ToolResult)
        
        Response = CallLLM(FullMessages, Tools=AllRegisteredTools)
    
    // Store in session
    Session.AddMessages(FullMessages)
    
    return Response.Message
```

---

### **9. Tool Registration/Definition**

**Methods to provide tools:**

1. **Constructor parameter** (immediate tools):
   ```csharp
   var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
       chatClient, 
       tools: new List<AITool> { tool1, tool2 }
   );
   ```

2. **ChatOptions.Tools** (per-invocation):
   ```csharp
   var options = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(
       new ChatOptions { Tools = new List<AITool> { tool1 } }
   );
   await agent.RunAsync("message", session, options, ct);
   ```

3. **ChatClientAgentOptions** (configured):
   ```csharp
   var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
   {
       ChatOptions = new ChatOptions 
       { 
           Tools = new List<AITool> { ... } 
       }
   };
   var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions);
   ```

---

### **10. Key Security Considerations**

From XML documentation warnings:

1. **Hallucination**: LLM output can be factually incorrect - always verify
2. **Indirect prompt injection**: Tool outputs may contain adversarial content
3. **Malicious payloads**: Sanitize LLM output before rendering/executing
4. **Tool invocation**: By default, all tools are invoked without user approval
5. **Untrusted input**: Function arguments from LLM should be validated

---

### **Key File Locations**
- Core abstractions: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.3.0/lib/net10.0/`
- Implementations: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.3.0/lib/net10.0/`
- Extensions.AI tools: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/10.5.0/lib/net10.0/`

### **8. The Agent Loop Pattern**

**Simplified Pseudocode:**
```
Session = CreateSession()

repeat:
    Messages = GetInputMessages()
    
    // Load history
    FullMessages = LoadChatHistory(Session) + Messages
    
    // Invoke LLM
    Response = CallLLM(FullMessages, Tools=AllRegisteredTools)
    
    // Dispatch loop
    while Response.Message.Type == "ToolCall":
        foreach ToolCall in Response.Message.ToolCalls:
            ToolResult = InvokeTool(ToolCall)
            FullMessages.Add(ToolResult)
        
        Response = CallLLM(FullMessages, Tools=AllRegisteredTools)
    
    // Store in session
    Session.AddMessages(FullMessages)
    
    return Response.Message
```

1. **Constructor parameter** (immediate tools):

   ```csharp
       chatClient, 
       tools: new List<AITool> { tool1, tool2 }
   );
   ```

2. **ChatOptions.Tools** (per-invocation):
   ```csharp
   var options = new ChatClientAgentRunOptions(
       new ChatOptions { Tools = new List<AITool> { tool1 } }
   );
   await agent.RunAsync("message", session, options, ct);
   ```

3. **ChatClientAgentOptions** (configured):
   ```csharp
   var agentOptions = new ChatClientAgentOptions
   {
       ChatOptions = new ChatOptions 
       { 
           Tools = new List<AITool> { ... } 
       }
   };
   var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, agentOptions);
   ```

### **10. Key Security Considerations**

From XML documentation warnings:

1. **Hallucination**: LLM output can be factually incorrect - always verify
2. **Indirect prompt injection**: Tool outputs may contain adversarial content
3. **Malicious payloads**: Sanitize LLM output before rendering/executing
4. **Tool invocation**: By default, all tools are invoked without user approval
5. **Untrusted input**: Function arguments from LLM should be validated

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Report: Microsoft.Extensions.AI Tool/Function System (v10.5.0)

### **Core Classes & Interfaces**

#### **1. AIFunction Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents a function that can be described to an AI service and invoked
- **Base Class:** Derives from `AIFunctionDeclaration`

**Constructors:**
- `AIFunction()` - Parameterless constructor

**Properties:**
- `UnderlyingMethod` (Property) - Gets the underlying `System.Reflection.MethodInfo` (returns null for non-.NET methods)
- `JsonSerializerOptions` (Property) - Gets `JsonSerializerOptions` for marshaling function parameters

**Methods:**
- `InvokeAsync(AIFunctionArguments arguments, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `Task<object>`
  - Invokes the function and returns its result
- `InvokeCoreAsync(AIFunctionArguments arguments, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `Task<object>`
  - Core invocation method
- `AsDeclarationOnly()` → `AIFunctionDeclaration`
  - Creates a non-invokable declaration-only representation

---

#### **2. AIFunctionDeclaration Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents a function that can be described to an AI service (base for AIFunction)
- **Base class for:** AIFunction

**Constructors:**
- `AIFunctionDeclaration()` - Parameterless constructor

**Properties:**
- `JsonSchema` (Property) - Gets JSON Schema describing the function and its input parameters
  - Contains self-contained JSON schema document with properties and required fields
  - Automatically derived from method parameters when created via AIFunctionFactory
- `ReturnJsonSchema` (Property) - Gets JSON Schema describing the function's return value
  - Automatically derived from return type
  - `null` for void/Task/ValueTask returns

---

#### **3. AITool Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents a tool that can be specified to an AI service

**Constructors:**
- `AITool()` - Parameterless constructor

**Properties:**
- `Name` (Property) - Gets the name of the tool
- `Description` (Property) - Gets description suitable for describing purpose to a model
- `AdditionalProperties` (Property) - Gets additional properties associated with the tool
- `DebuggerDisplay` (Property) - Gets string to display in debugger

**Methods:**
- `GetService(Type serviceType, object serviceKey)` → object
  - Retrieves object of specified type; returns null if not found
- `GetService<TService>(object serviceKey)` → TService
  - Generic version for strongly-typed service retrieval
- `ToString()` - Override for string representation

---

#### **4. AIFunctionArguments Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents arguments to be used with AIFunction.InvokeAsync()
- **Implements:** `IDictionary<string, object>` and related interfaces
- **Description:** Dictionary of name/value pairs with additional metadata

**Constructors:**
- `AIFunctionArguments()`
  - Parameterless, uses default ordinal comparer
- `AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object> arguments)`
  - Contains specified arguments
- `AIFunctionArguments(IEqualityComparer<string> comparer)`
  - Specifies custom comparer for key comparisons
- `AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object> arguments, IEqualityComparer<string> comparer)`
  - Contains arguments with custom comparer

**Properties:**
- `Services` (Property) - Gets or sets `IServiceProvider` optionally associated with arguments
- `Context` (Property) - Gets or sets dictionary of arbitrary name/value pairs for use by AIFunction
- Dictionary properties: `Item[]`, `Keys`, `Values`, `Count`, `IsReadOnly`

**Dictionary Methods:**
- `Add(string key, object value)`
- `Clear()`
- And standard dictionary operations

---

#### **5. AIFunctionFactory Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Provides factory methods for creating `AIFunction` instances from .NET methods
- **Key Feature:** Automatically derives JSON schemas for parameters and return types

**Factory Methods (Create overloads):**

##### **From Delegate:**
1. `Create(Delegate method, AIFunctionFactoryOptions options)` → `AIFunction`
   - Creates from delegate with options for metadata/behavior overrides

```
    ↓
```

- `GetService(Type serviceType, object serviceKey)` → object
  - Retrieves object of specified type; returns null if not found
- `GetService<TService>(object serviceKey)` → TService
  - Generic version for strongly-typed service retrieval
- `ToString()` - Override for string representation

---

#### **4. AIFunctionArguments Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents arguments to be used with AIFunction.InvokeAsync()
- **Implements:** `IDictionary<string, object>` and related interfaces
- **Description:** Dictionary of name/value pairs with additional metadata

**Constructors:**
- `AIFunctionArguments()`
  - Parameterless, uses default ordinal comparer
- `AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object> arguments)`
  - Contains specified arguments
- `AIFunctionArguments(IEqualityComparer<string> comparer)`
  - Specifies custom comparer for key comparisons
- `AIFunctionArguments(IDictionary<string, object> arguments, IEqualityComparer<string> comparer)`
  - Contains arguments with custom comparer

**Properties:**
- `Services` (Property) - Gets or sets `IServiceProvider` optionally associated with arguments
- `Context` (Property) - Gets or sets dictionary of arbitrary name/value pairs for use by AIFunction
- Dictionary properties: `Item[]`, `Keys`, `Values`, `Count`, `IsReadOnly`

**Dictionary Methods:**
- `Add(string key, object value)`
- `Clear()`
- And standard dictionary operations

---

#### **5. AIFunctionFactory Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Provides factory methods for creating `AIFunction` instances from .NET methods
- **Key Feature:** Automatically derives JSON schemas for parameters and return types

**Factory Methods (Create overloads):**

##### **From Delegate:**
1. `Create(Delegate method, AIFunctionFactoryOptions options)` → `AIFunction`
   - Creates from delegate with options for metadata/behavior overrides
   
2. `Create(Delegate method, string name, string description, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions)` → `AIFunction`
   - Creates from delegate with explicit name, description, and serializer options

##### **From MethodInfo (Static Methods):**
3. `Create(MethodInfo method, object target, AIFunctionFactoryOptions options)` → `AIFunction`
   - `target` should be null for static methods
   - `target` required for instance methods (this is the object instance)

4. `Create(MethodInfo method, object target, string name, string description, JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions)` → `AIFunction`
   - Static version with explicit metadata

##### **From MethodInfo with Custom Target Factory:**
5. `Create(MethodInfo method, Func<AIFunctionArguments, object> targetFactory, AIFunctionFactoryOptions options)` → `AIFunction`
   - Allows dynamic target creation per invocation

##### **Declaration Only:**
6. `CreateDeclaration(string name, string description, JsonElement jsonSchema, JsonElement? returnJsonSchema)` → `AIFunctionDeclaration`
   - Creates non-invokable declaration for description purposes

**Parameter Binding:**
- `CancellationToken` parameters → automatically bound to invocation token (excluded from schema)
- `IServiceProvider` parameters → bound from `AIFunctionArguments.Services` (excluded from schema)
- `AIFunctionArguments` parameters → bound directly (excluded from schema)
- Other parameters → bound from arguments dictionary (included in schema)

**Return Value Handling:**
- Default: Serialized to `JsonElement` using `JsonSerializerOptions`
- Special cases (not serialized): `AIContent`, derived types, `IEnumerable<AIContent>`
- Can be customized via `AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult`

---

#### **6. AIFunctionFactoryOptions Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Options for creating AIFunction from methods

**Constructors:**
- `AIFunctionFactoryOptions()`

**Properties:**
- `SerializerOptions` (Property) - Gets/sets `JsonSerializerOptions`
  - Default: `AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions`
  
- `JsonSchemaCreateOptions` (Property) - Gets/sets `AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions`
  - Governs JSON schema generation for parameters and return type
  - Default: `AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions.Default`
  
- `Name` (Property) - Gets/sets function name
  - Default: Derived from DisplayNameAttribute or method name
  
- `Description` (Property) - Gets/sets function description
  - Default: Derived from DescriptionAttribute
  
- `AdditionalProperties` (Property) - Gets/sets additional values for `AITool.AdditionalProperties`
  
- `ConfigureParameterBinding` (Property) - Gets/sets delegate for custom parameter binding
  - Delegate: `Func<ParameterInfo, ParameterBindingOptions>`
  - Not invoked for CancellationToken parameters
  
- `MarshalResult` (Property) - Gets/sets delegate for custom result marshaling
  - Delegate: `Func<object, Type, object>`
  - Provides complete control over result marshaling
  
- `ExcludeResultSchema` (Property) - Gets/sets whether to exclude return type schema generation
  - Default: `false`

**Nested Type:**
- `ParameterBindingOptions` - Configuration for individual parameter binding
  - `BindParameter` (Property) - Delegate for custom parameter binding
  - `ExcludeFromSchema` (Property) - Whether to exclude from schema

---

#### **7. ChatOptions Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents options for a chat request

**Constructors:**
- `ChatOptions()`
  - Parameterless initialization
- `ChatOptions(ChatOptions other)`
  - Shallow copy of all properties

**Properties (Relevant to Tools):**
- `Tools` (Property) - Gets/sets `IList<AITool>` - list of tools to include with request
  - This is where AIFunction instances are passed to IChatClient
  
- `AllowMultipleToolCalls` (Property) - Gets/sets bool
  - `true`: No limit on tool calls per response
  - `false`: Maximum of one tool call per request
  - `null`: Provider selects default

**Other Configuration Properties:**
- `ConversationId` (Property) - Optional identifier for existing conversation
- `Instructions` (Property) - Additional per-request instructions
- `Temperature` (Property) - Controls randomness (lower = less random)
- `MaxOutputTokens` (Property) - Maximum tokens in response
- `TopP` (Property) - Nucleus sampling factor
- `TopK` (Property) - Most probable tokens considered
- `FrequencyPenalty` (Property) - Penalty for repeated tokens
- `PresencePenalty` (Property) - Penalty based on existing presence
- `Seed` (Property) - Seed for reproducibility
- `Reasoning` (Property) - Reasoning options for request
- `ResponseFormat` (Property) - Response format (Text/Json/JsonSchema)
- `ModelId` (Property) - Model ID for request
- `StopSequences` (Property) - List of stop sequences
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` (Property) - Allow long-running background operations

---

#### **8. IChatClient Interface**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions`
- **Summary:** Represents a chat client interface
- **Thread Safety:** Thread-safe for concurrent use unless stated otherwise

**Methods:**
1. `GetResponseAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ChatOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `Task<ChatResponse>`
   - Sends chat messages and returns response
   - `messages` - Sequence of chat messages to send
   - `options` - Chat options with request configuration
   - Returns: Response messages generated by client
   - Throws: `ArgumentNullException` if messages is null

2. `GetStreamingResponseAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ChatOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<ChatResponseUpdate>`
   - Sends chat messages and streams response
   - Returns: Streaming response messages

3. `GetService(Type serviceType, object serviceKey)` → object
   - Retrieves strongly-typed services provided by client
   - Used to access `ChatClientMetadata` and other services
   - Returns: Found object or null

---

#### **9. FunctionInvokingChatClient Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Summary:** Delegating chat client that automatically invokes functions defined in ChatOptions
- **Features:**
  - Processes `FunctionCallContent` and invokes corresponding `AIFunction`
  - Produces `FunctionResultContent` and sends back to inner client
  - Loops until no more function calls or stop condition met
  - Handles `ApprovalRequiredAIFunction` by replacing with `ToolApprovalRequestContent`
  - Passes through `AIFunctionDeclaration` (non-invokable) instances

**Constructors:**
- `FunctionInvokingChatClient(IChatClient innerClient, ILoggerFactory loggerFactory, IServiceProvider functionInvocationServices)`
  - `innerClient` - Underlying IChatClient or next in chain
  - `loggerFactory` - For logging function invocation info
  - `functionInvocationServices` - Optional IServiceProvider for resolving AIFunction services

**Properties:**
- `FunctionInvocationServices` (Property) - Gets IServiceProvider specified during construction
- `CurrentContext` (Property) - Gets/sets FunctionInvocationContext for current invocation
- `Processor` (Property) - Gets function invocation processor (lazy-created)
- `IncludeDetailedErrors` (Property) - Include detailed error information
- `AllowConcurrentInvocation` (Property) - Allow multiple concurrent function invocations
- `MaximumIterationsPerRequest` (Property) - Maximum function call loop iterations
- `AdditionalTools` (Property) - Additional tools beyond those in ChatOptions

**Methods:**
- `GetResponseAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ChatOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `Task<ChatResponse>`
- `GetStreamingResponseAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage> messages, ChatOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<ChatResponseUpdate>`

---

#### **10. ChatClientBuilder Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Summary:** Builder for creating pipelines of IChatClient instances

**Constructors:**
- `ChatClientBuilder(IChatClient innerClient)`
  - Initialize with inner client as backend
- `ChatClientBuilder(Func<IServiceProvider, IChatClient> innerClientFactory)`
  - Initialize with factory function for inner client

**Methods:**
- `Build(IServiceProvider services)` → `IChatClient`
  - Builds final IChatClient representing entire pipeline
  - Returns: Instance representing entire pipeline
  
- `Use(Func<IChatClient, IChatClient> clientFactory)` → `ChatClientBuilder`
  - Adds factory for intermediate chat client
  
- `Use(Func<IChatClient, IServiceProvider, IChatClient> clientFactory)` → `ChatClientBuilder`
  - Adds factory with service provider
  
- `Use(Func<IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, ChatOptions, Func<...>, CancellationToken, Task> sharedFunc)` → `ChatClientBuilder`
  - Adds anonymous delegating client with shared implementation
  
- `Use(Func<...GetResponseAsync...>, Func<...GetStreamingResponseAsync...>)` → `ChatClientBuilder`
  - Adds with separate non-streaming and streaming implementations

---

#### **11. FunctionInvokingChatClientBuilderExtensions Class**
**Location:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`
- **Summary:** Extension methods for attaching FunctionInvokingChatClient to pipeline

**Methods:**
- `UseFunctionInvocation(ChatClientBuilder builder, ILoggerFactory loggerFactory, Action<FunctionInvokingChatClient> configure)` → `ChatClientBuilder`
  - Enables automatic function call invocation
  - `builder` - ChatClientBuilder being used
  - `loggerFactory` - Optional logger factory
  - `configure` - Optional callback to configure FunctionInvokingChatClient
  - Returns: Supplied builder

---

### **Key Integration Points**

**Tool Registration Flow:**
```
AIFunctionFactory.Create(method, options)
    ↓
AIFunction (with JsonSchema for params, ReturnJsonSchema)
    ↓
ChatOptions.Tools.Add(aiFunction)
    ↓
IChatClient.GetResponseAsync(messages, options)
    ↓
FunctionInvokingChatClient processes FunctionCallContent
    ↓
Invokes matching AIFunction from ChatOptions.Tools
    ↓
Returns FunctionResultContent
```

**Working with Static Methods:**
```csharp
AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    typeof(MyClass).GetMethod("StaticMethod"),
    target: null,  // null for static
    options: new AIFunctionFactoryOptions { ... }
)
```

**Working with Instance Methods:**
```csharp
var instance = new MyClass();
AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    typeof(MyClass).GetMethod("InstanceMethod"),
    target: instance,  // Required for instance methods
    options: new AIFunctionFactoryOptions { ... }
)
```

**Building Chat Pipeline:**
```csharp
var builder = new ChatClientBuilder(innerClient);
builder.UseFunctionInvocation(loggerFactory);
var client = builder.Build(serviceProvider);
```

---

### **Special Cases & Behaviors**

**Parameter Binding Precedence:**
1. CancellationToken → Always from invocation token (not in schema)
2. IServiceProvider → From AIFunctionArguments.Services (not in schema)
3. AIFunctionArguments → Direct binding (not in schema)
4. Others → From arguments dictionary (included in schema)

**Return Value Marshaling:**
- Default: JSON serialization to JsonElement
- Special (as-is): AIContent, derived types, IEnumerable<AIContent>
- Customizable via MarshalResult delegate

**Function Approval:**
- ApprovalRequiredAIFunction instances convert to ToolApprovalRequestContent
- Requires ToolApprovalResponseContent in subsequent request
- When any function requires approval, all functions in response require approval

2. **Architecture:**

   - Use `AIFunctionFactory.Create` to create tools from methods
   - Use `IChatClient` from MEAI with OpenAI provider

I'll organize this as separate tool classes and wire everything together in Program.cs, keeping the console app structure clean and maintainable.

For the web search functionality, I need to decide between a simulated approach or using a real API. Since this is a demo, I'll make it practical by implementing a search tool that can work with actual HTTP requests to fetch and parse web content, with the flexibility to use a real search API if credentials are available or fall back to simulated results for a self-contained example.
